stories.title: Minister declines to say whether Burnham will heed Lineker’s call for wealth tax
stories.summary: A Treasury minister has declined to say whether Andy Burnham’s Government will heed a call from millionaires including Gary Lineker for a higher tax on their wealth. Chief Secretary to the Treasury Emma Reynolds welcomed the offer, but said any announcement on tax policy would be made in the budget. Sports pundit and former footballer Lineker is among 120 UK-based millionaires to sign an open letter to the Prime Minister saying they should pay more tax. The “Proud to Pay” letter published on Thursday claims the money raised from higher taxes on wealth could be used to reduce inequality, support public infrastructure, and back small businesses.

stories.title: Watch the First Trailer for Pulp’s New Film What Do You Do for an Encore?
stories.summary: Watch the First Trailer for Pulp’s New Film What Do You Do for an Encore? This fall, Mubi will release Pulp: What Do You Do for an Encore? , a 90-minute film narrated by the band’s own Jarvis Cocker. Directed by their longtime collaborator Garth Jennings, the feature combines footage from their arena tour behind last year’s reunion album, More, with archival film tracing the band’s rise from tenacious Sheffield also-rans to Britpop darlings. Watch the first trailer below. Though the Mubi-exclusive streaming date has not yet been revealed, viewers in the United Kingdom, Ireland, and North America can catch one-night-only previews in September.
entities: What Do You Do for an Encore?

stories.title: Imperious Hegarty named The Sunday Game Hurler of Year
stories.summary: The Sunday Game have chosen Gearoid Hegarty as their Hurler of the Year with dominant All-Ireland champions Limerick taking up no less than 10 positions on the show's Team of the Year. Hegarty was at his imperious best as the Treaty County romped past Galway in Sunday's showpiece at Croke Park and he forms one-third of an all-Limerick half-forward line in our best 15 of the 2026 season. Beaten finalists Galway take up three spots on the final selection with one each for Clare and Cork. The Sunday Game Team of the Year 1. Nickie Quaid (Limerick) Another banner year for Limerick's last line of defence.

stories.title: ‘Up in smoke’: Attacks on ‘Russia’s Amazon’ bring Putin’s war closer to home
stories.summary: A series of Ukrainian strikes on the warehouses of Russia’s largest online retailer has sent potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in inventory up in flames, sparking an online furor among the country’s embattled small-business owners. Over the weekend, Ukrainian drones hit Wildberries distribution centers in Elektrostal in the Moscow region and Kotovsk in the Tambov region. A second wave of attacks early Wednesday hit logistics warehouses in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions, while state media in the southwestern Voronezh region also reported that a drone strike forced the closure of a Wildberries warehouse there late Wednesday into Thursday.

stories.title: Sami Sheen says dad Charlie ‘hasn’t given me a dime’ — touts OnlyFans to support herself
stories.summary: Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen, shut down rumors that her famous father funds her luxurious lifestyle, while crediting OnlyFans for her success. “Look, if I had Daddy’s money, I would own it, OK? I would let you guys know,” the model shared via a TikTok video Saturday in response to a comment that the actor gives her money. “These comments make me so frustrated because this man hasn’t given me a dime in over four years. I have been fully financially supporting myself since I was 18 years old,” she said. Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen (seen above with her dad, mom Denise Richards and sister Lola), addressed rumors that the actor pays for her lifestyle.

stories.title: More than 80,700 sign petition calling for World Cup final replay
stories.summary: Argentina's defeat to Spain in the 2026 World Cup final, played on Sunday 19 July at New Jersey's MetLife Stadium, has sparked a wave of protests that continues several days after the final whistle. Fan Gisela Sánchez launched a petition on Change. org calling on FIFA to replay the match, which was decided by a Ferran Torres goal in extra time. At the time of writing, the petition had gathered more than 80,739 signatures. The petition accuses Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic of delivering a "controversial and corrupt" performance and calls on the governing body to review the decisions made during the match.
stories.trend: argentina fans launched a petition demanding a world cup replay

stories.title: 'No adverse findings' that alumina exports are going to Russian arms factories, says minister
stories.summary: Aughinish Alumina plant on the Shannon Estuary in Co Limerick RollingNews. ie Breaking aughinish alumina Peter Burke said there is “a lack of verifiable internal trade data within Russia” to corroborate the claims. “NO ADVERSE FINDINGS have been determined” in a review of alleged breaches of sanctions by Aughinish Alumina in Co Limerick, the minister for enterprise has said. Peter Burke, whose department is responsible for enforcing such sanctions, said the independent review looked at news reports about a potential leakage of alumina from the Russian-owned plant into military supply chains.

stories.title: Iran: Call to Save the Life of Amir Hassan Akbari-Monfared - National Council of Resistance of Iran
stories.summary: The clerical regime’s judiciary has sentenced political prisoner Amir Hassan Akbari-Monfared, currently held in Evin Prison, to death on the charge of “rebellion (Baghy) through contact with the People’s Mojahedin Organization of Iran (PMOI/MEK). ” This retaliatory sentence was issued by Branch 15 of Tehran’s notorious Revolutionary Court, presided over by the executioner Abolqasem Salavati, solely due to his family relationship with the martyred PMOI member Farshid Asadi, in connection with the “Abdar-chi” (tea dispenser) case and the elimination of executioners Razini and Moqiseh.
stories.sourceName: National Council of Resistance of Iran - NCRI
sources: National Council of Resistance of Iran - NCRI

stories.title: Love Island's Priya is branded 'vile, classist and tone deaf' for 'job-shaming' Elicia over £30k National Rail career - as viewers call for her dismissal from show
stories.summary: Love Island's Priya Jaswal has been branded 'vile, classist and tone deaf' for 'job-shaming' her co-star Elicia Bailey's £30,000 job at National Rail on last night's show. The Surrey-based business development manager, 25, was left raging after seeing a clip of Rotherham-born bombshell Elicia, 23, calling her the 'biggest game player' on spin-off show Aftersun. Priya, whose role could see her earn around £43,000 based on national averages, took aim at Elicia’s job as a team organiser on last night’s show, saying: 'I don't care what Elicia says. I don't care. It's cringy. 'Go back to the National Rail. Elicia, you work for the National Rail, why are you f***ing sitting in Aftersun?

stories.title: Weekend weather set to change following warm spell
stories.summary: Ireland’s weekend weather will turn unsettled with scattered rain and drizzle as a warm spell ends. Temperatures will range from 15 to 24 degrees, staying above normal but breezier by Saturday. Rain will be heaviest in the west and north, easing slightly in the southeast.

stories.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
stories.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

stories.title: Milan’s Rafael Leao: Intermediaries trying to iron Galatasaray transfer
stories.summary: Serie A side Milan are set to part ways with Rafael Leao and attempts are growing to possibly secure a move to Galatasaray for the Portuguese. Daniele Longo has reported that a move to Gala is slowly starting to tempt Leao, who has asked for time to think about a move. He is still not completely sure but there are signs he could now agree to join at some point in the future. This is especially because moves to Aston Villa or Tottenham haven’t quite taken off. The two clubs were offered Leao and memorised the terms but are currently considering other targets.

stories.title: Wildfire emergency: France requests EU support as 23,000 evacuated in Biscarrosse wildfire crisis
stories.summary: Firefighting aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to arrive soon to aid French emergency crews. France has formally asked for assistance from other European Union countries as it battles devastating wildfires in the southwest of the country. President Emmanuel Macron confirmed that France has activated the European Union Civil Protection Mechanism, seeking urgent support, particularly for aerial firefighting. Aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, the Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to join the efforts in the coming days. https://x. com/EmmanuelMacron/status/2080421021006405681?

stories.title: Míchel: 'Ajax are interested in him. But he is not there yet'
stories.summary: Pro Shots Jul 24, 2026 01:53-05:00 Translated by Míchel could only laugh on Thursday when Ziggo Sport asked him about Marc-André ter Stegen. The Ajax manager could not hide the club's interest in the Barcelona goalkeeper. "That news is known to everyone," Míchel said with a smile shortly before the European meeting with FK Vojvodina. "He is a player we are interested in. But he is not here yet. " He then shifted the focus to the options already in the building. "The only thing I can say is that I am happy with the goalkeepers who are here. I will keep working to improve Ajax. As for the transfers, Jordi Cruyff knows what we need," the coach said, passing the ball to Ajax's technical director.

stories.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
stories.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected Secretary General of the PSOE. At that time, he could not have imagined that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting on the set of Mañaneros on TVE giving explanations for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have arrived earlier.

stories.title: UK takes command of multinational force for Ukraine
stories.summary: Lieutenant General Tom Bateman has taken command of the multinational force for Ukraine, succeeding his French predecessor in a ceremony at Paris' operation headquarters, as Britain and France reaffirm their commitment to supporting Ukraine's security through upcoming exercises.

stories.title: Man Utd mascot, six, hit by club's ticket tout crackdown
stories.summary: Ruari's mum denies any wrongdoing and says she manages other family members' tickets for them. Image source, Family photo Image caption, Katie said Ruairi has held a season ticket since birth By Haider Saleem North West A mother has said her six-year-old son has had restrictions put on his Manchester United season ticket as part of the club's anti-touting crackdown. Katie, 39, from Urmston, Trafford, said she had been left unable to apply for an away game ticket for her "football daft" son Ruari - who had once been a match mascot - ahead of the Premier League opener against Hull.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: The UK’s new prime minister has revived the National Economic Council, giving regional mayors a leading role in economic policy decisions under the No 10 North plans. The council’s first meeting on Friday will include senior ministers and mayors to prioritise devolution and address regional disparities. The move aims to shift decision-making power from London to drive growth across the country.

stories.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
stories.summary: I mran Perretta always knew that the title of his debut feature film would be “Ish” – short for Ishmail, the name of the 12-year-old boy from Luton at the heart of his story. Only later did people point out the similarity to other coming-of-age films with three letters in the title: Kes by Ken Loach, and Satyajit Ray’s Apu trilogy. Now, as the reviews trickle in, the comparisons to Kes and Apu keep coming. Like them, Ish is a poetic film that grabs you by the heart and pulls you into a child’s perspective on the world. The response has been lovely, says Perretta, and a relief. “It’s nice that people have compared Ish to those films rather than Kidulthood. I did worry about that.

stories.title: M4 closure causes long delays after police stop all traffic
stories.summary: News Latest Wales News M4 LIVE EVENT ENDED Updated 19 hours ago Traffic officers brought the motorway to a halt. Traffic has been brought to a standstill on the M4 in Newport (Image: Traffic Wales) Updated 13:03, 23 Jul 2026 Trafficwas brought to a standstill on the M4 in Newport on Thursday morning as emergency works are being carried out on the westbound carriageway. At its peak, traffic maps displayed 50 minute delays at Junction 25, Caerleon, for all drivers travelling towards Cardiff. Drivers were urged to allow extra time for their journeys.

stories.title: Code in iOS 27 lets Apple deactivate leased iPhones, report says
stories.summary: The iPhone 18 is about to launch, along with the final version of its operating system, iOS 27, most likely in September. But a new report says it may arrive with a feature that lets Apple, and third-party lenders, remotely deactivate phones if customers fall behind on payments. With the iOS 27 beta now widely available, 9to5Mac found code that would let Apple place delinquent iPhones in "Restricted Mode. " This would limit users to a short list of widgets and apps, including the App Store, Clock, Settings, Wallet, and Passwords, according to the report.

stories.title: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it?
stories.summary: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it? Remember DeepSeek? The Chinese artificial intelligence startup that rattled the US tech industry with its model that nearly matched leading rivals, trained at a fraction of the cost? China may have done it again. The latest Chinese AI model making waves was developed by a lesser-known startup called Moonshot AI. In just two days after launch, Moonshot suspended new subscriptions after demand overwhelmed its computing capacity.

stories.title: Eerst regen op komst, daarna kans op tropische hitte
stories.summary: A cooler spell brought temperatures between 20 and 25 degrees across the Netherlands and parts of Scandinavia, Germany, Austria and the Balkans last week. Storms are expected to develop at the boundary of cold and warm air in the coming days, with highs rising to 28 degrees on Saturday before heavy rain and cooler conditions return Sunday.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking the start of the prime minister’s plan to decentralise power from London. Two senior ministers and up to 300 civil servants will join him by next year as part of the push to boost regional growth. The National Economic Council will discuss devolution and local investment, aiming to address long-standing disparities across the UK.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of peninsula as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials on Friday morning saying it had continued to progress overnight. Authorities in France have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula (Lège-Cap Ferret) in the south-west of France, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat. The tourism hot spot is being evacuated as firefighters continue to battle a wildfire that has raged in the Gironde region for days. The fire, which began on Tuesday in Saumos, continued its progression overnight with 8,700 hectares (around 21,500 acres) consumed by the flames.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

stories.title: Tourist tax introduced in Edinburgh: How much is it and who pays?
stories.summary: Edinburgh has become Scotland’s first city to introduce a 5% tourist tax on overnight stays, starting Friday. The levy aims to raise about £50 million annually for housing, culture and policing. Opponents warn it could deter some visitors.

stories.title: What's on for Team NI on first day of Commonwealth Games?
stories.summary: Team Northern Ireland kicks off the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow with action starting at 08:30 BST as Gary Kelly takes on New Zealand’s Shannon McIlroy in the men’s singles. Swimmers Victoria Catterson, Grace Davison and Lottie Cullen lead the morning heats at Tollcross Swimming Centre, with semi-finals and finals scheduled for 19:00. One of Team NI’s boxers, Louis Rooney, will also compete at the SEC Centre.

stories.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
stories.summary: Landlords say high costs of running hospitality business outweigh potential savings of business rates discount One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. The policy, announced on Thursday, is part of a broader agenda to support the high street and will cost £100m. Continue reading. One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. So how does the policy measure up in the context of running a business and where does the money on your bar bill actually go?

stories.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Indonesian studio Visinema will release the fantasy epic Queen of Malacca in early 2027, following post-production and rising international interest from distributors in China and Thailand. The sequel to the hit Jumbo is part of the company’s expansion plans for its Southeast Asian franchise.

stories.title: Almost €19m paid in prison staff allowances in 2025
stories.summary: Almost €19 million in allowances was paid to Irish prison staff in 2025, with €15.7 million going to 3,684 employees as operational allowances. Smaller sums covered specific roles, including €64,000 for tuck shop workers and €27,000 for dog handlers, while high-security prison staff received environment allowances.

stories.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
stories.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney. Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result.

stories.title: Adidas-Aktie: Unternehmen schwächelt
stories.summary: Adidas shares fell 1.73% to €176.15 at Thursday’s Xetra close, extending a downward trend after closing at €179.25 the day before. The DAX index dropped 1.77% overall as 476,062 Adidas stocks traded, down from 604,052 the prior session.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: Protest leaders from India’s Cockroach Janta Party will meet government officials on Friday as nationwide demonstrations demanding the education minister’s resignation enter their fourth week. Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike after police forcibly removed him from the protest site and hospitalized him last Saturday. The government has pledged tougher laws on exam paper leaks, but protesters vow to continue until Minister Dharmendra Pradhan steps down.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Frankreich lässt russischen Schattentanker nach Verstoß gegen Flaggenregel weiterfahren
stories.summary: French authorities released the Russian-flagged oil tanker Deliver from Marseille on Thursday after fining it for violating flag regulations. The ship, suspected of transporting Russian oil in breach of sanctions, left French waters after payment. France has now detained four vessels from Russia’s shadow fleet, two of which were fined.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted in Somerset, marking its first confirmed UK sighting in years. The bird was filmed at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate, where conservationists have restored freshwater habitats to support wildlife. Conservationists say the sighting highlights the success of wetland restoration efforts in reversing habitat loss.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric models cut hospital admissions and missed work or school by 70% for people with poorly controlled asthma. The project in Cleveland, Ohio, involved 1,200 participants but ended early due to funding cuts, leaving over 1,100 households without the intervention. Researchers reported asthma improvements comparable to or better than standard medication trials.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Female veterans’ support groups are gaining national traction as former RAF medic Chelley Tulloch reveals how PTSD from multiple war-zone deployments nearly broke her, underscoring the urgent need for dedicated mental health services. Tulloch, now a vocal advocate, says women entering the military will require far more support, with groups across the UK uniting to fill the gap. The Ministry of Defence confirms increased investment in NHS veteran services to address rising demand.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

entityShots.name: Supreme Court of the United States

entities: Supreme Court of the United States

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the European Commission for breaching the EU’s Digital Markets Act by favouring its own apps over rivals. The fine follows two separate violations, including one €460m penalty for unfairly promoting Google’s services in search results. Google warns the ruling could strip key features for European users, but EU officials insist the penalties protect fair competition.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

offbeat.title: Minister declines to say whether Burnham will heed Lineker’s call for wealth tax
offbeat.summary: A Treasury minister has declined to say whether Andy Burnham’s Government will heed a call from millionaires including Gary Lineker for a higher tax on their wealth. Chief Secretary to the Treasury Emma Reynolds welcomed the offer, but said any announcement on tax policy would be made in the budget. Sports pundit and former footballer Lineker is among 120 UK-based millionaires to sign an open letter to the Prime Minister saying they should pay more tax. The “Proud to Pay” letter published on Thursday claims the money raised from higher taxes on wealth could be used to reduce inequality, support public infrastructure, and back small businesses.

offbeat.title: Watch the First Trailer for Pulp’s New Film What Do You Do for an Encore?
offbeat.summary: Watch the First Trailer for Pulp’s New Film What Do You Do for an Encore? This fall, Mubi will release Pulp: What Do You Do for an Encore? , a 90-minute film narrated by the band’s own Jarvis Cocker. Directed by their longtime collaborator Garth Jennings, the feature combines footage from their arena tour behind last year’s reunion album, More, with archival film tracing the band’s rise from tenacious Sheffield also-rans to Britpop darlings. Watch the first trailer below. Though the Mubi-exclusive streaming date has not yet been revealed, viewers in the United Kingdom, Ireland, and North America can catch one-night-only previews in September.

I would let you guys know,” the model shared via a TikTok video Saturday in response to a comment that the actor gives her money. “These comments make me so frustrated because this man hasn’t given me a dime in over four years. I have been fully financially supporting myself since I was 18 years old,” she said. Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen (seen above with her dad, mom Denise Richards and sister Lola), addressed rumors that the actor pays for her lifestyle.

The petition accuses Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic of delivering a "controversial and corrupt" performance and calls on the governing body to review the decisions made during the match.

offbeat.title: Love Island's Priya is branded 'vile, classist and tone deaf' for 'job-shaming' Elicia over £30k National Rail career - as viewers call for her dismissal from show
offbeat.summary: Love Island's Priya Jaswal has been branded 'vile, classist and tone deaf' for 'job-shaming' her co-star Elicia Bailey's £30,000 job at National Rail on last night's show. The Surrey-based business development manager, 25, was left raging after seeing a clip of Rotherham-born bombshell Elicia, 23, calling her the 'biggest game player' on spin-off show Aftersun. Priya, whose role could see her earn around £43,000 based on national averages, took aim at Elicia’s job as a team organiser on last night’s show, saying: 'I don't care what Elicia says. I don't care. It's cringy. 'Go back to the National Rail. Elicia, you work for the National Rail, why are you f***ing sitting in Aftersun?

offbeat.title: Weekend weather set to change following warm spell
offbeat.summary: Ireland’s weekend weather will turn unsettled with scattered rain and drizzle as a warm spell ends. Temperatures will range from 15 to 24 degrees, staying above normal but breezier by Saturday. Rain will be heaviest in the west and north, easing slightly in the southeast.

offbeat.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
offbeat.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

offbeat.title: Milan’s Rafael Leao: Intermediaries trying to iron Galatasaray transfer
offbeat.summary: Serie A side Milan are set to part ways with Rafael Leao and attempts are growing to possibly secure a move to Galatasaray for the Portuguese. Daniele Longo has reported that a move to Gala is slowly starting to tempt Leao, who has asked for time to think about a move. He is still not completely sure but there are signs he could now agree to join at some point in the future. This is especially because moves to Aston Villa or Tottenham haven’t quite taken off. The two clubs were offered Leao and memorised the terms but are currently considering other targets.

offbeat.title: Wildfire emergency: France requests EU support as 23,000 evacuated in Biscarrosse wildfire crisis
offbeat.summary: Firefighting aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to arrive soon to aid French emergency crews. France has formally asked for assistance from other European Union countries as it battles devastating wildfires in the southwest of the country. President Emmanuel Macron confirmed that France has activated the European Union Civil Protection Mechanism, seeking urgent support, particularly for aerial firefighting. Aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, the Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to join the efforts in the coming days. https://x. com/EmmanuelMacron/status/2080421021006405681?

offbeat.title: Míchel: 'Ajax are interested in him. But he is not there yet'
offbeat.summary: Pro Shots Jul 24, 2026 01:53-05:00 Translated by Míchel could only laugh on Thursday when Ziggo Sport asked him about Marc-André ter Stegen. The Ajax manager could not hide the club's interest in the Barcelona goalkeeper. "That news is known to everyone," Míchel said with a smile shortly before the European meeting with FK Vojvodina. "He is a player we are interested in. But he is not here yet. " He then shifted the focus to the options already in the building. "The only thing I can say is that I am happy with the goalkeepers who are here. I will keep working to improve Ajax. As for the transfers, Jordi Cruyff knows what we need," the coach said, passing the ball to Ajax's technical director.

offbeat.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
offbeat.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected Secretary General of the PSOE. At that time, he could not have imagined that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting on the set of Mañaneros on TVE giving explanations for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have arrived earlier.

offbeat.title: UK takes command of multinational force for Ukraine
offbeat.summary: Lieutenant General Tom Bateman has taken command of the multinational force for Ukraine, succeeding his French predecessor in a ceremony at Paris' operation headquarters, as Britain and France reaffirm their commitment to supporting Ukraine's security through upcoming exercises.

offbeat.title: M4 closure causes long delays after police stop all traffic
offbeat.summary: News Latest Wales News M4 LIVE EVENT ENDED Updated 19 hours ago Traffic officers brought the motorway to a halt. Traffic has been brought to a standstill on the M4 in Newport (Image: Traffic Wales) Updated 13:03, 23 Jul 2026 Trafficwas brought to a standstill on the M4 in Newport on Thursday morning as emergency works are being carried out on the westbound carriageway. At its peak, traffic maps displayed 50 minute delays at Junction 25, Caerleon, for all drivers travelling towards Cardiff. Drivers were urged to allow extra time for their journeys.

offbeat.title: Code in iOS 27 lets Apple deactivate leased iPhones, report says
offbeat.summary: The iPhone 18 is about to launch, along with the final version of its operating system, iOS 27, most likely in September. But a new report says it may arrive with a feature that lets Apple, and third-party lenders, remotely deactivate phones if customers fall behind on payments. With the iOS 27 beta now widely available, 9to5Mac found code that would let Apple place delinquent iPhones in "Restricted Mode. " This would limit users to a short list of widgets and apps, including the App Store, Clock, Settings, Wallet, and Passwords, according to the report.

offbeat.title: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it?
offbeat.summary: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it? Remember DeepSeek? The Chinese artificial intelligence startup that rattled the US tech industry with its model that nearly matched leading rivals, trained at a fraction of the cost? China may have done it again. The latest Chinese AI model making waves was developed by a lesser-known startup called Moonshot AI. In just two days after launch, Moonshot suspended new subscriptions after demand overwhelmed its computing capacity.

offbeat.title: Eerst regen op komst, daarna kans op tropische hitte
offbeat.summary: A cooler spell brought temperatures between 20 and 25 degrees across the Netherlands and parts of Scandinavia, Germany, Austria and the Balkans last week. Storms are expected to develop at the boundary of cold and warm air in the coming days, with highs rising to 28 degrees on Saturday before heavy rain and cooler conditions return Sunday.

offbeat.title: Tourist tax introduced in Edinburgh: How much is it and who pays?
offbeat.summary: Edinburgh has become Scotland’s first city to introduce a 5% tourist tax on overnight stays, starting Friday. The levy aims to raise about £50 million annually for housing, culture and policing. Opponents warn it could deter some visitors.

offbeat.title: Almost €19m paid in prison staff allowances in 2025
offbeat.summary: Almost €19 million in allowances was paid to Irish prison staff in 2025, with €15.7 million going to 3,684 employees as operational allowances. Smaller sums covered specific roles, including €64,000 for tuck shop workers and €27,000 for dog handlers, while high-security prison staff received environment allowances.

offbeat.title: Adidas-Aktie: Unternehmen schwächelt
offbeat.summary: Adidas shares fell 1.73% to €176.15 at Thursday’s Xetra close, extending a downward trend after closing at €179.25 the day before. The DAX index dropped 1.77% overall as 476,062 Adidas stocks traded, down from 604,052 the prior session.

offbeat.title: Frankreich lässt russischen Schattentanker nach Verstoß gegen Flaggenregel weiterfahren
offbeat.summary: French authorities released the Russian-flagged oil tanker Deliver from Marseille on Thursday after fining it for violating flag regulations. The ship, suspected of transporting Russian oil in breach of sanctions, left French waters after payment. France has now detained four vessels from Russia’s shadow fleet, two of which were fined.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

sports.title: Man Utd mascot, six, hit by club's ticket tout crackdown
sports.summary: Ruari's mum denies any wrongdoing and says she manages other family members' tickets for them. Image source, Family photo Image caption, Katie said Ruairi has held a season ticket since birth By Haider Saleem North West A mother has said her six-year-old son has had restrictions put on his Manchester United season ticket as part of the club's anti-touting crackdown. Katie, 39, from Urmston, Trafford, said she had been left unable to apply for an away game ticket for her "football daft" son Ruari - who had once been a match mascot - ahead of the Premier League opener against Hull.

sports.title: What's on for Team NI on first day of Commonwealth Games?
sports.summary: Team Northern Ireland kicks off the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow with action starting at 08:30 BST as Gary Kelly takes on New Zealand’s Shannon McIlroy in the men’s singles. Swimmers Victoria Catterson, Grace Davison and Lottie Cullen lead the morning heats at Tollcross Swimming Centre, with semi-finals and finals scheduled for 19:00. One of Team NI’s boxers, Louis Rooney, will also compete at the SEC Centre.

sports.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
sports.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney. Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result.

politics.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
politics.summary: The UK’s new prime minister has revived the National Economic Council, giving regional mayors a leading role in economic policy decisions under the No 10 North plans. The council’s first meeting on Friday will include senior ministers and mayors to prioritise devolution and address regional disparities. The move aims to shift decision-making power from London to drive growth across the country.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking the start of the prime minister’s plan to decentralise power from London. Two senior ministers and up to 300 civil servants will join him by next year as part of the push to boost regional growth. The National Economic Council will discuss devolution and local investment, aiming to address long-standing disparities across the UK.

politics.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
politics.summary: Landlords say high costs of running hospitality business outweigh potential savings of business rates discount One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. The policy, announced on Thursday, is part of a broader agenda to support the high street and will cost £100m. Continue reading. One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. So how does the policy measure up in the context of running a business and where does the money on your bar bill actually go?

entertainment.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
entertainment.summary: I mran Perretta always knew that the title of his debut feature film would be “Ish” – short for Ishmail, the name of the 12-year-old boy from Luton at the heart of his story. Only later did people point out the similarity to other coming-of-age films with three letters in the title: Kes by Ken Loach, and Satyajit Ray’s Apu trilogy. Now, as the reviews trickle in, the comparisons to Kes and Apu keep coming. Like them, Ish is a poetic film that grabs you by the heart and pulls you into a child’s perspective on the world. The response has been lovely, says Perretta, and a relief. “It’s nice that people have compared Ish to those films rather than Kidulthood. I did worry about that.

entertainment.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
entertainment.summary: Indonesian studio Visinema will release the fantasy epic Queen of Malacca in early 2027, following post-production and rising international interest from distributors in China and Thailand. The sequel to the hit Jumbo is part of the company’s expansion plans for its Southeast Asian franchise.

breaking.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
breaking.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

breaking.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
breaking.summary: Protest leaders from India’s Cockroach Janta Party will meet government officials on Friday as nationwide demonstrations demanding the education minister’s resignation enter their fourth week. Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike after police forcibly removed him from the protest site and hospitalized him last Saturday. The government has pledged tougher laws on exam paper leaks, but protesters vow to continue until Minister Dharmendra Pradhan steps down.

breaking.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
breaking.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

facts.title: France orders evacuation of peninsula as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
facts.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials on Friday morning saying it had continued to progress overnight. Authorities in France have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula (Lège-Cap Ferret) in the south-west of France, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat. The tourism hot spot is being evacuated as firefighters continue to battle a wildfire that has raged in the Gironde region for days. The fire, which began on Tuesday in Saumos, continued its progression overnight with 8,700 hectares (around 21,500 acres) consumed by the flames.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted in Somerset, marking its first confirmed UK sighting in years. The bird was filmed at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate, where conservationists have restored freshwater habitats to support wildlife. Conservationists say the sighting highlights the success of wetland restoration efforts in reversing habitat loss.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric models cut hospital admissions and missed work or school by 70% for people with poorly controlled asthma. The project in Cleveland, Ohio, involved 1,200 participants but ended early due to funding cuts, leaving over 1,100 households without the intervention. Researchers reported asthma improvements comparable to or better than standard medication trials.

health.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
health.summary: Female veterans’ support groups are gaining national traction as former RAF medic Chelley Tulloch reveals how PTSD from multiple war-zone deployments nearly broke her, underscoring the urgent need for dedicated mental health services. Tulloch, now a vocal advocate, says women entering the military will require far more support, with groups across the UK uniting to fill the gap. The Ministry of Defence confirms increased investment in NHS veteran services to address rising demand.

crisis.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
crisis.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

crisis.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

entityShots.name: Supreme Court of the United States

entities: Supreme Court of the United States

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the European Commission for breaching the EU’s Digital Markets Act by favouring its own apps over rivals. The fine follows two separate violations, including one €460m penalty for unfairly promoting Google’s services in search results. Google warns the ruling could strip key features for European users, but EU officials insist the penalties protect fair competition.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.
